ip_outcome_note: Depicted pink bipedal creature with Shellder clamped on arm like a cannon matches Galarian Slowbro's canonical design exactly — the Galarian form's defining visual distinction (Shellder on arm vs. Kanto Shellder on tail). National Pokedex no. 80. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-99).

trivia_bullets.text: Galarian Slowbro is a regional variant introduced in the Isle of Armor DLC (June 2020) for Sword & Shield. Galarian Slowpoke has been eating Galarica spices, and when a Shellder clamps onto its arm instead of its tail, the spice residue causes a chemical reaction producing a potent poison. Galarian Slowbro is Poison/Psychic-type rather than the Kanto form's Water/Psychic. Evolution requires a Galarica Cuff, crafted from eight Galarica Twigs found on the Isle of Armor. [WebSearch snippet: GameSpot Galarian Slowbro evolution guide]
sources: WebSearch snippet: GameSpot/Dexerto Galarian Slowbro evolution

trivia_bullets.text: The Shellder on Galarian Slowbro's arm functions like a ranged weapon: Slowbro grabs the Shellder's tongue to trigger it, firing poisonous liquid at speeds described as rivaling the fastest known reflexes — a sharp contrast to the canonical Slowbro's reputation for being oblivious and slow. The TCG attack Tripping Shot (130 damage, locks the Defending Pokemon from retreating) and the Rapid-Fire Poison ability echo this "gun-arm" combat design. [WebSearch snippet: pokejungle.net Galarian Slowpoke origin of species; PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-99]

As a Pokemon V (Basic V, 210 HP), Galarian Slowbro V gives up 2 Prize cards when knocked out. The Rapid-Fire Poison ability provides free passive poisoning every turn it is Active, creating a pressure mechanic even when the opponent stalls. Market price $1.21 as of the 2026-05-13 CSV — the highest-valued card in this trivia wave. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-99]
No flavor text on this card — V-rank cards in the Sword & Shield era typically omit flavor text in favor of the V Rule reminder text. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-99]
